records.ety: [OE. yung, yong, ong, ung, AS. geong; akin to OFries. iung, iong, D. joing, OS., OHG., & G. jung, Icel. ungr, Sw. & Dan. ung, Goth. juggs, Lith. jaunas, Russ. iunuii, L. juvencus, juvenis, Skr. juvaça, juvan. √281. Cf. Junior, Juniper, Juvenile, Younker, Youth.]

defs: Not long born; still in the first part of life; not yet arrived at adolescence, maturity, or age; not old; juvenile; -- said of animals; as, a young child; a young man; a young fawn.
quotes: For he so young and tender was of age.
“Whom the gods love, die young,” has been too long carelessly said; . . . whom the gods love, live young forever.

defs: Being in the first part, pr period, of growth; as, a young plant; a young tree.
quotes: While the fears of the people were young.

defs: Having little experience; inexperienced; unpracticed; ignorant; weak.
quotes: Come, come, elder brother, you are too young in this.

defs: The offspring of animals, either a single animal or offspring collectively.
quotes: [The egg] bursting with kindly rupture, forth disclosed Their callow young.
